title: 10 medicinal constituents of Cicuta virosa (water hemlock)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Cicuta virosa cicutin is a highly toxic plant species found in North America and Europe. It belongs to the carrot family and is often mistaken for wild parsley or carrots due to its similar appearance. Cicutin contains a deadly neurotoxin called cicutoxin, which can cause seizures, muscle paralysis, and even death if ingested.

Cicuta virosa anhydrocicutoxin is a potent neurotoxin found in the plant's leaves, stems, and seeds. This highly toxic compound is responsible for the plant's notorious reputation as one of the most deadly plants in North America. Anhydrocicutoxin can cause severe symptoms including numbness, tingling, muscle weakness, and respiratory failure if ingested or absorbed through skin contact. It has a rapid onset of action and is often fatal within 15-60 minutes after exposure.

Cicuta virosa cicutoxin is a highly toxic compound found in all parts of the plant. This potent neurotoxin can cause severe symptoms including numbness, tingling, muscle weakness, and respiratory failure if ingested. Cicutoxin acts by disrupting the normal functioning of nerves, leading to muscle contractions and eventual paralysis.

Cicuta virosa cicutolactone is a highly toxic lactone isolated from the roots of the plant. It has been reported to possess a number of bioactive properties, including antitumor and antimicrobial activities. Cicutolactone has also been shown to exhibit significant cytotoxicity against various human cancer cell lines, making it a potential candidate for further investigation as an anticancer agent.

Cicuta virosa virosic acid is a toxic compound found in the plant's leaves and stems. It is a powerful neurotoxin that can cause paralysis of the nervous system, leading to respiratory failure and death if ingested in significant amounts. Virosic acid is responsible for the high toxicity of the plant, making it one of the most poisonous plants known.

Cicuta virosa cicutine is a highly toxic plant that belongs to the carrot family. It grows in wet environments and its tall stalks can reach up to 3 meters high. Cicutine is considered one of the most deadly plants in the world, with just a small amount capable of causing severe neurological damage or even death. Its toxic compounds are released through its leaves, stems, and seeds.

Cicuta virosa cicutenolide is a toxic alkaloid found in various parts of the plant. It is responsible for the severe toxicity of the plant, causing symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, seizures, and even death if ingested. Cicutenolide has been studied for its potential medicinal applications, particularly in cancer treatment, although it remains highly regulated due to its extreme toxicity.

Cicuta virosa cicutochalcone is a naturally occurring compound found in the plant's roots and leaves. It is a potent bioactive molecule that has been traditionally used in medicine for its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. Cicutochalcone has been shown to exhibit antioxidant activity, inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules and providing protection against oxidative stress. Its potential therapeutic applications are being explored, including treating various inflammatory diseases and conditions.

Cicuta virosa virosol is a highly toxic plant species that belongs to the carrot family. It contains a potent neurotoxin called cicutoxin, which can cause severe symptoms including seizures, vomiting, and respiratory failure if ingested. The plant grows in damp environments and can be mistaken for other edible plants, making it a significant risk to human health.

Cicuta virosa 6-hydroxycicutoxin is a toxic compound isolated from the plant's roots. It is a potent neurotoxin that can cause severe symptoms including muscle weakness, respiratory failure, and cardiac arrhythmias. The toxin works by blocking sodium channels in nerve cells, leading to paralysis and eventual death. Despite its toxicity, 6-hydroxycicutoxin has been studied for potential use as a treatment for certain neurological disorders, such as multiple sclerosis.
